english: sessing a quality in a high degree, dug-drag-pa (dug drag pa) Stg. very poisonous. — 4. symb. num. 11. Comp. drag-nád (drag nad), v. dreg-nád (dreg nad), gout. — drág-rtsal-càn (drag rtsal can) = drág-po (drag po), of deities. — drag-źán (drag zhan) strong and weak, e.g. the relative force of sound Gram.; also high and low, with respect to rank. — drag-śul (drag shul) frightfulness, drag-śul-càn (drag shul can) frightful, terrible, powerful; cruel, frq., yet chiefly with respect to the power manifested by gods and sorcerers. — drag-gśed (drag gshed) lit. 'cruel hangman', a terrifying deity v. Schl. 111, 214.

adv. very, much, greatly, maṅ-drags (mang drags) Mil. very much; adj. much, strong, intense, bza-btuṅ-drags (bza' btung drags) eating and drinking a great deal S.g.; dran-drags (dran drags) an intense, most vivid, remembrance of a person Mil., an ardent longing or desire; dga-drags-nas (dga' drags nas) being very happy, highly rejoiced Pth, C.; gyod-drags-nas (gyod drags nas) feeling deep repentance Mil.; bsten drags-na (bsten drags na) if one continues it too long S.g.

straight 1. not deviating from the direct course, not crooked or oblique, t́ig (thig), lam (lam) etc. frq.; lus draṅ-po jóg-pa (lus drang po 'jog pa) to sit straight; *ḱa búb-ne dáṅ-po ćo-će* (*kha bub ne dang po cho che*) W. to place a thing straight or upright again; *t́éd-la dáṅ-po* (*thed la dang po*) W., horizontal. — 2. right, e.g. lam (lam), opp. to log-pa (log pa). — 3. sincere, honest, upright, truthful, draṅ-poi raṅ-bźin-can-gyi ṕyir (drang po'i rang bzhin can gyi phyir) because they have an upright character Dzl.; las draṅ-po (las drang po) good actions, righteous deeds, opp. to rtsub-po (rtsub po) violent, unjust Stg.; ḱrims draṅ-po (khrims drang po) 1. a just sentence, righteous judgment, opp. to log-pa (log pa). — 2. applied to men, with regard to their acting according to justice and the law (v. ḱrims (khrims)); ćos-draṅ-po (chos drang po) honest, upright, with respect to religion and the divine law; also draṅ-po (drang po) alone, whenever it is not to be misunderstood, may be used for our just. — draṅ-por (drang por), tsig draṅ-por smrá-ba (tshig drang por smra ba) to be candid, to speak the truth, frq. draṅ-don (drang don) v. ṅes-pa (nges pa) extr.
1. abstract noun to draṅ-po (drang po). 2. pf. to drén-pa (dren pa).

1. a holy hermit, an order of men, introduced from Brahmanism into Buddhism. These saints are looked upon partly as human beings, partly as Dewas, and at any rate as being endowed with miraculous powers Dzl. frq. — 2. At present the Lama that offers sbyin-sreg (sbyin sreg) is stated to bear that name, and whilst he is attending to the sacred rites, he is not allowed to eat anything but dkar-zas (dkar zas) (v. dkar-po (dkar po)). — 3. symb. num.: 7.
Lh. the beam of a pair of scales, Hind. डंडी.

I. vb. 1. to think of, c. accus., with or without yid-la (yid la), gen. to think of past events, to remember, recollect, call to mind, drin (drin) benefits, v. drin (drin); byuṅ-ba-rnams (byung ba rnams) that which has happened Glr.; more emphatically: rjés-su drán-pa (rjes su dran pa) frq.; but also dkon-mćóg drán-pa (dkon mchog dran pa) to think of, to remember, God; sdúg-po yóṅ-ba de ma drán-pa yin (sdug po yong ba de ma dran pa yin) do not think of, do not trouble yourself about, future evils Mil.; bskyis-par mi drán-no (bskyis par mi dran no) I do not recollect having taken anything on credit Dzl.; drán-pa tsám-gyis (dran pa tsam gyis) as soon as one thinks of it, quick as thought Thgr.; so-só-nas... drán-par gyis śig (so so nas... dran par gyis shig) every body should think of... Dzl. (the simple imp. seems not to be used); (rjés-su) drán-par byéd-pa (rjes su dran par byed pa) also: to remind of, to put in mind of, to revive the memory of, = drán-du júg-pa (dran du 'jug pa), dran-skúl byéd-pa (dran skul byed pa) Lex. — 2. to become conscious of, to recollect, rmi-lam (rmi lam) a dream Pth.; drán-par gyúr-ba (dran par gyur ba) to recover one's senses, to be one's self again Dzl.; ciaṅ mi drán-pa (ci ang mi dran pa) insensible Dzl.; mi drán-pai óg-tu (mi dran pa'i 'og tu) after they had become insensible Dzl. — 3. to think of with love or affection, to be attached to, to long for, ă-ma (a ma) for the mother col.; *dran-sém* (*dran sem*) W. love, affection, attachment; dran-mćog-rje (dran mchog rje) dearest Sir! Mil. II. sbst. 1. remembrance, recollection, reminiscence; memory frq.; drán-pa gsál-po (dran pa gsal po) a retentive memory. — 2. consciousness, stor (stor) is lost; t́ugs dran-méd-du t́im-pa (thugs dran med du thim pa) to lose one's senses, resp. Mil.; dran-méd-du brgyál-pa (dran med du brgyal pa) id.; drán-pa rnyéd- (dran pa rnyed)
